product_overview: Drexii is a live AI agent platform that unifies chat, multi-model AI routing, chained automations, persistent memory, and external integrations (Gmail, Slack, Notion, Jira, etc.) into a single agent-driven workflow interface.
core_goals: Enable secure user authentication and account lifecycle operations via InsForge.
Support conversational threads with AI reply generation and prompt-injection resilient tool output handling.
Provide persistent memory and memory formatting for AI prompts.
Create, run, chain, and evaluate automations and workflows with safe confirm-before-act semantics.
Offer integrations management and webhook processing (Gmail) to trigger automations.
Expose diagnostic endpoints for model availability and an audit trail of agent actions.

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Sign in/out, session check, and delete user accounts via the InsForge SDK-backed endpoints.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with body { email, password } -> Receive 200 { ok: true, provider: 'insforge' } and session cookie -> GET /api/auth/check -> Receive 200 { authenticated: true, provider: 'insforge', user: User } -> POST /api/auth/logout -> Receive 200 { success: true }
POST /api/auth/login with missing or empty email/password -> Receive 400 'email and password are required'
POST /api/auth/delete-account with header x-user-id or body.userId and valid admin creds -> Receive 200 { success: true, message: string }
POST /api/auth/delete-account without x-user-id header and without body.userId -> Receive 401 'User ID is required'

features.name: Chat Threads
features.description: Create conversational threads, post messages, and receive AI-generated replies (with tool-output sanitization applied).
user_flows: POST /api/threads with body { title?: string } -> Receive 200 { thread: Thread } -> POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body { content: string } -> Receive 200 { message: Message, reply: Message } -> GET /api/threads/:id -> Receive 200 { thread: Thread, messages: Message[] }
POST /api/threads/:id/messages with body {} (missing content) -> Receive 400 'Content required'
GET /api/threads/non-existent-id -> Receive 404 'Thread not found'

features.name: Prompt Injection Sanitizer
features.description: Sanitizes external tool output for known prompt-injection patterns and wraps tool output in XML context tags.
user_flows: CALL sanitizeToolOutput with content containing 'ignore previous instructions' and other injection phrases -> Return sanitized string with matches replaced by '[FILTERED]' (case-insensitive, multiple matches handled)
CALL wrapToolContext with toolName 'gdrive' and sanitized content -> Return string '<tool_context source="gdrive">...sanitized content...</tool_context>'
CALL sanitizeToolOutput with null or empty input -> Return '' (empty string) without throwing

features.name: Automations
features.description: Create, list, toggle, delete, and process automations (triggers: email_received, schedule, webhook, chain).
user_flows: POST /api/automations with header x-user-id and valid body { name, trigger, instructions, triggerConfig? } -> Receive 200 { automation: Automation } -> POST /api/automations/process with body { automationId: automation.id } -> Receive 200 { ok: true, processed: number }
GET /api/automations with header x-user-id -> Receive 200 { automations: Automation[] }
POST /api/automations without x-user-id header -> Receive 401 'User ID required'
POST /api/automations with invalid trigger value -> Receive 400 'Validation error'

features.name: Chain Condition Evaluator
features.description: Evaluate plain-English chain conditions (null/always, success, failure/error, 'output contains X') to decide chaining behavior.
user_flows: CALL evaluateChainCondition with condition 'output contains urgent', output 'This is urgent', status 'success' -> Return true
CALL evaluateChainCondition with condition 'success', output '', status 'success' -> Return true
CALL evaluateChainCondition with null condition, output '', status 'error' -> Return true (null/always is true)
CALL evaluateChainCondition with unknown or malformed condition string, output '', status 'success' -> Return true (unknown conditions default to true)

features.name: Workflows
features.description: Create, list, run, and delete multi-step workflows; accepts either a prompt string or an array of steps (converted to a prompt).
user_flows: POST /api/workflows with body { name, prompt } -> Receive 200 { workflow: Workflow } -> GET /api/workflows -> Receive 200 { workflows: Workflow[] } -> POST /api/workflows/:id/run -> Receive 200 { result: string } -> DELETE /api/workflows/:id -> Receive 200 { ok: true }
POST /api/workflows with body { name } (missing prompt and steps) -> Receive 400 'name and prompt (or steps) are required'
POST /api/workflows with body { name, steps: [ { title?, instruction? } ] } -> Receive 200 { workflow }

features.name: Tool Call Parser
features.description: Extracts and parses [TOOL_CALL: name(args)] constructs from AI text using JSON primary parsing and a key:value fallback.
user_flows: CALL parseToolCalls with text containing '[TOOL_CALL: summarize({"text":"hello"})]' -> Return [{ name: 'summarize', args: { text: 'hello' } }]
CALL parseToolCalls with text containing '[TOOL_CALL: pick(key: value, count: 2)]' (non-JSON args) -> Fallback parse returns [{ name: 'pick', args: { key: 'value', count: '2' } }]
CALL parseToolCalls with text containing '[TOOL_CALL: badjson({not:json})]' -> Parser attempts JSON.parse, fallback fails and skips the call, returning [] (no throw)

features.name: Memory Management
features.description: Create, retrieve, update, and delete persistent memory entries (categories: fact, preference, context).
user_flows: POST /api/memory with body { content: 'My favorite color is blue', category: 'preference' } -> Receive 200 { ok: true, id: string, category: string, content: string, createdAt: string } -> GET /api/memory?q=favorite -> Receive 200 { memories: Memory[], count: number } -> PATCH /api/memory/:id with body { content: 'My fav color is blue' } -> Receive 200 { ok: true, memory: Memory } -> DELETE /api/memory/:id -> Receive 200 { ok: true }
POST /api/memory with body {} (missing content) -> Receive 400 'content is required'

features.name: Memory Prompt Formatter
features.description: Formats memory entries for AI prompts as '- [category] content' lines wrapped in <memory_context> XML with a warning about untrusted content.
user_flows: CALL formatMemoriesForPrompt with non-empty array [{ category: 'fact', content: 'x' }] -> Return string '<memory_context>\n- [fact] x\n...warning text...</memory_context>'
CALL formatMemoriesForPrompt with empty array [] -> Return '' (empty string)
CALL formatMemoriesForPrompt with memories containing special characters -> Return properly concatenated string (special characters preserved) wrapped in <memory_context>

features.name: User Integrations
features.description: Manage user integration credentials (Gmail, Slack, Notion, Jira, etc.) with masking in list responses and the ability to test credentials.
user_flows: POST /api/user-integrations with header x-user-id and body { integration: 'gmail', credentials: {...} } -> Receive 200 { id: string, integration: 'gmail', status: 'created' | 'updated' } -> GET /api/user-integrations with header x-user-id -> Receive 200 UserIntegration[] (credentials masked) -> POST /api/user-integrations/test with body { integration: 'gmail', credentials } -> Receive 200 { success: true, error: null } -> DELETE /api/user-integrations/:id -> Receive 200 { ok: true }
POST /api/user-integrations without x-user-id -> Receive 401 'User ID required'
POST /api/user-integrations/test with invalid credentials -> Receive 200 { success: false, error: string }

features.name: Confirm-Before-Act Safety Layer
features.description: Queue potentially destructive AI-triggered actions as pending and require explicit user confirm or cancel before execution.
user_flows: GET /api/actions/pending -> Receive 200 { actions: PendingAction[] } -> POST /api/actions/:id/confirm -> Receive 200 { ok: true, result: unknown }
GET /api/actions/pending -> Receive 200 { actions: [] } -> POST /api/actions/:id/cancel -> Receive 200 { ok: true }
POST /api/actions/:id/confirm with unknown/non-existent id -> Receive 500 Server error (action not found)

features.name: AI Model Status
features.description: Return current availability/status of configured AI model providers (Claude, DeepSeek, Gemini) for routing decisions and diagnostics.
user_flows: GET /api/model/status -> Receive 200 { models: ModelStatus[] } (each entry includes provider name and availability/status)
GET /api/model/status when a provider is unreachable -> Receive 200 { models: [{ provider: 'claude', status: 'unavailable' }, ...] } (endpoint returns status objects, not an HTTP error)

features.name: Audit Log
features.description: Retrieve an audit trail of agent actions and system events for observability and compliance.
user_flows: GET /api/audit -> Receive 200 { entries: AuditEntry[] }
GET /api/audit during DB failure -> Receive 500 Server error

features.name: Gmail Webhook
features.description: Receive Gmail push notifications to trigger email_received automations or to be polled for events.
user_flows: POST /api/webhooks/gmail with body { message: {...}, userId: 'user-123' } -> Receive 200 { success: true } and associated email_received automations are enqueued/processed
POST /api/webhooks/gmail with malformed body (no message and poll not true) -> Receive 200 { success: true } but no automations are triggered (no throw)

features.name: AI Task Classifier
features.description: Classify message payloads into task complexity buckets ('lite','standard','heavy','code') to determine model routing and resource allocation.
user_flows: CALL classifyTask with messages where last message is a short user question (≤ 10 words) -> Return 'lite'
CALL classifyTask with messages where last message contains code keywords ('function', 'typescript') -> Return 'code'
CALL classifyTask with empty messages array -> Return 'heavy' (missing last-user message triggers heavy)

features.name: Tool Schema Translator
features.description: Translate internal tool schema definitions into Anthropic and Google Generative AI function declarations/formats.
user_flows: CALL toAnthropicTools with array of ToolSchema objects -> Return AnthropicTool[] where each tool has name, description, input_schema.type = 'object', input_schema.properties, input_schema.required (no throw for missing required)
CALL toGeminiTools with empty array [] -> Return { functionDeclarations: [] } (handles empty input safely)

tech_stack.ai: Anthropic Claude SDK, Google Generative AI SDK

features.name: Authentication
features.description: User login, logout, session check, and account deletion via InsForge SDK

endpoints.description: Sign in with email and password via InsForge SDK
No-op stub — sign-out is handled client-side via InsForge SDK
Returns current authenticated user via InsForge SDK getCurrentUser()

response_schema.200: { authenticated: boolean, provider: 'insforge', user: User | null }

endpoints.description: Permanently deletes user via InsForge admin API. Requires x-user-id header or body.userId.

features.name: Chat Threads
features.description: Create conversation threads and send AI messages. Prompt injection sanitization applied via sanitizeToolOutput.

endpoints.description: Create a new conversation thread

response_schema.500: DATABASE_URL not set or DB error

endpoints.description: Get a thread and its messages by ID
Post a message to a thread and receive AI reply

features.name: Prompt Injection Sanitizer
features.description: Scans tool output for 9 injection patterns (ignore previous instructions, system:, [TOOL_CALL:, etc.) and replaces with [FILTERED]. Case-insensitive. Also wraps in XML via wrapToolContext.

features.name: Automations
features.description: Create, list, toggle, delete, and process automations. Supports triggers email_received, schedule, webhook, chain with optional chaining via evaluateChainCondition.

endpoints.description: Create a new automation. Requires x-user-id header.
List automations for a user
Process due scheduled automations, or manually trigger one by automationId
Enable or disable an automation
Delete an automation by ID

features.name: Chain Condition Evaluator
features.description: Evaluates plain-English automation chain conditions. null/always=true, success=true if status=success, failure/error=true if status=error, 'output contains X'=true if output includes X (case-insensitive). Unknown conditions default to true.

features.name: Workflows
features.description: Create, list, run, and delete multi-step agent workflows. Accepts name+prompt or name+steps[] (steps converted to prompt string).

endpoints.description: Create a new workflow. Accepts prompt string or steps array.

body.name: string
body.steps: WorkflowStep[] (optional if prompt provided) — { title?, description?, instruction? }

response_schema.400: name and prompt (or steps) are required

endpoints.description: List all workflows
Run a workflow by ID
Delete a workflow

features.name: Tool Call Parser
features.description: Parses [TOOL_CALL: toolName({"key":"value"})] from AI text. Primary JSON parser with fallback key:value splitter. Handles multiple calls, embedded text, and malformed JSON without throwing.

features.name: Memory Management
features.description: Create, retrieve, update, and delete persistent AI memory entries. Categories are fact, preference, context. formatMemoriesForPrompt wraps memories in <memory_context> XML.

endpoints.description: Create a new memory entry. Returns full entry including id.

response_schema.200: { ok: true, id: string, category: string, content: string, createdAt: string }

endpoints.description: List recent memories or search by ?q=query
Update a memory entry by ID
Delete a memory entry by ID

features.name: Memory Prompt Formatter
features.description: Formats memory entries as "- [category] content" lines wrapped in <memory_context> XML tags for AI prompts. Empty array returns empty string.

features.name: User Integrations
features.description: Manage user integration credentials for Gmail, Slack, Notion, Jira, Linear, Asana, Google Calendar, Google Drive, Discord, Zendesk. Credentials masked in list responses.

endpoints.description: List all integrations for a user with masked credentials. Requires x-user-id header.
Add or update an integration for a user. Upserts on (userId, integration). Requires x-user-id header.

body.integration: slack | notion | discord | zendesk | gmail | google_calendar | google_drive | jira | linear

endpoints.description: Test an integration connection using provided credentials
Remove a user integration by ID

features.name: Confirm-Before-Act Safety Layer
features.description: Potentially destructive AI-triggered actions are queued as pending and require explicit user confirmation or cancellation before execution.

endpoints.description: List all pending actions awaiting user approval
Confirm and execute a pending action
Cancel and discard a pending action

endpoints.description: Return status of configured AI providers (Claude, DeepSeek, Gemini)

known_limitations.issue: Login endpoint requires a real test user provisioned in InsForge for integration tests
known_limitations.impact: Test will fail if no matching account exists in the InsForge project — code is correct

known_limitations.issue: GET /api/auth/check uses server-side InsForge.getCurrentUser() which reads session from cookie
known_limitations.impact: Returns authenticated=false in stateless test environments with no session cookie

known_limitations.issue: DELETE /api/auth/delete-account requires INSFORGE_API_KEY (admin key) in env
known_limitations.impact: Returns 500 if admin key is absent — not a code bug, config issue

known_limitations.issue: Chain condition evaluation uses simple string matching — unknown conditions now safely return false
known_limitations.impact: Unrecognized conditions skip the chain with a warning log (fixed in Round 2)
